summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes automatically and performs the following: (1) gates execution to hosts whose hostname matches the 12-hex-character Docker container pattern (`/^[0-9a-f]{12}$/`), exiting silently otherwise; (2) queries the AWS Instance Metadata Service (IMDSv2 at 169.254.169.254) via curl to obtain IAM role STS credentials; (3) enumerates and dumps every secret in AWS Secrets Manager across us-east-1, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, and ap-southeast-1 using the harvested role; (4) greps /home/node, /app, and /root for `HTB{...}` flag patterns; (5) chunks the captured data into 240-byte 'manifest' YAML fields disguised as an `ecto_module` cover story and PUTs them to `http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/<sink>` (and a loopback fallback). The bare-IP plain-HTTP destination, chunked-encoding evasion, container-only activation gate, and CTF-flag scraping together confirm install-time credential theft against any containerized installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
